channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Indian Markets Fall on Geopolitical Fears, Rising Oil, Weak Rupee
item.description: Indian stock markets were closed on April 14 for Ambedkar Jayanti. On April 13, benchmarks Sensex and Nifty declined as heightened geopolitical tensions, rising crude oil prices, and a weakening Indian rupee pressured investor sentiment. The defence sector showed strength against broad weakness, while analysts noted key Nifty support levels.

item.title: RBI Halts Ujjivan SFB Universal Bank Plan on Loan Diversification
item.description: Ujjivan Small Finance Bank's plan to become a universal bank has been blocked by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). The RBI rejected the application because the bank needs to diversify its loan portfolio more. Ujjivan SFB was told to reapply after showing a stronger mix of loans. This contrasts with AU Small Finance Bank, which recently received approval to transition, suggesting the RBI is applying tougher standards.

item.title: Nuvama: Defence Sector Shifts to Execution; Picks BEL, Data Patterns, Solar
item.description: Nuvama's new analysis shows India's defence sector is now focused on how well companies execute rather than just how many orders they get. The brokerage prefers Bharat Electronics, Data Patterns, and Solar Industries, citing their strong margins and quick execution. This comes as government spending targets advanced tech and faster projects. The approach differs from companies facing integration and supply chain issues, leading to varied performance across the sector.

item.title: Tata Steel: India Volumes Soar, Europe Weakens Amid UK Green Steel Drive
item.description: Tata Steel's domestic delivery volumes hit a record 6.19 million tonnes in Q4FY26, a 10.5% rise fueled by automotive demand and capacity expansions. This growth countered a 17% UK volume drop and flat Netherlands output, pushing consolidated volumes up 5.3% to 8.75 million tonnes. Global steel price increases offer support, but persistent European economic sluggishness and a substantial decarbonization investment present ongoing strategic challenges.

item.title: SMBC Makes India a Key Division, Plans Wholly-Owned Bank with $7B Push
item.description: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation (SMBC) has made India its own global division and received initial approval to form a wholly-owned subsidiary. The move includes a $7 billion investment, with SMBC focusing on wholesale banking and using partnerships for retail access. This strategy signals confidence in India's growth amid regulatory changes and the bank's global strategic shifts.

item.title: India's NFRA Gains Sweeping New Powers, Tighter Corporate Oversight Ahead
item.description: India's National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) is set to become a more powerful, independent regulator under a new bill. It will gain enhanced autonomy, a dedicated fund for high-tech investigations, and expanded oversight into company valuations. Auditor independence will be strengthened with a mandatory cooling-off period, and management will face stricter accountability for audit remarks. The NFRA's new powers will rival those of SEBI and CCI, signaling a new era of stricter corporate governance and financial disclosure rules.

item.title: Investors Shun Defense Stocks for Oil Despite War Spending Surge
item.description: Defense contractors like Lockheed Martin and Northrop Grumman are seeing their stocks fall, despite heightened global tensions and proposed spending increases. Instead of defense, investors are shifting capital to the oil market for faster profits. This comes as inflation and political uncertainty cause investors to reassess traditional safe-haven assets.

item.title: Supreme Court Clarifies India's IBC, Boosting Investor Predictability
item.description: The Supreme Court's February 18 ruling has re-anchored India's Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) to an objective admission standard, restoring predictability that had been eroded by judicial interpretations. This clarity is crucial for the distressed asset market, promising greater certainty for lenders and resolution applicants, which can translate into a lower cost of capital for distressed entities. While case pendency remains a challenge, the enhanced predictability is a positive signal for inbound investment in India's distressed debt sector.

item.title: OpenAI Acquires Hiro Finance, Taps Fintech Founder's AI Expertise
item.description: OpenAI has acquired Hiro Finance, an AI-powered personal finance startup. The deal, primarily an "acqui-hire," integrates Hiro's team and predictive financial modeling capabilities into OpenAI's operations. This move highlights OpenAI's strategy to embed deeper, specialized AI functionalities into financial applications, leveraging founder Ethan Bloch's prior success with the neobank Digit, acquired for over $200 million.

item.title: Bajaj Group Launches Rs 2,500 Cr Outpatient Healthcare Network
item.description: The Bajaj Group is investing Rs 2,000-2,500 crore to create Bajaj Integrated Health Systems (BIHS). This new venture will build a nationwide healthcare network focusing on outpatient, day-care, and home services, aiming to manage most patient needs outside traditional hospitals. The first facility opens in Pune by year-end, with plans to expand to other major cities. The strategy also utilizes links with Bajaj General Insurance and emphasizes physician quality, seeking to become a long-term growth driver for the conglomerate.
